topic: Graphing Linear Inequalities and Solving Systems

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Slope Conversion
misconceptions.description: Some students interpret the coefficients in inequalities as the slope without converting them to slope-intercept form. This leads them to graph equations with incorrect slopes, resulting in misrepresentation of inequalities.
successIndicators: Identify boundary line as dashed or solid

misconceptions.title: Inclusive vs. Exclusive Boundaries
misconceptions.description: The error occurs when inequalities are graphed with solid lines regardless of whether they are inclusive. Misunderstanding leads to incorrectly including or excluding the boundary in the solution set.
successIndicators: Draw solid lines for inclusive inequalities
Draw dashed lines for strict inequalities
Shade appropriately based on inequality type

misconceptions.title: Switching X and Y Coordinates
misconceptions.description: In solving systems of equations, students swap x and y coordinates, leading to incorrect solutions. This misunderstanding results from confusion over variable placement in the ordered pair.
successIndicators: Check order of (x, y) in solutions

misconceptions.title: Failure to Clear Fractions
misconceptions.description: Errors arise when students do not correctly clear fractions from equations. This may lead to unnecessary complexity or incorrect combining of terms, causing inaccurate solutions.
